echo "PID: $!" && sleep 25 && echo "=== Tunnel URL ===" && curl -s --max-time 10 http://localhost:4040/api/tunnels 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "

d=json.load(sys.stdin)
for t in d.get('tunnels',[]):

" 2>/dev/null && echo "" && echo "=== Test public reachability ===" && curl -s --max-time 15 -o /dev/null -w "HTTP=%{http_code} TIME=%{time_total}s SIZE=%{size_download}\n" "https://vauq5ee-anonymous-8083.exp.direct" 2>/dev/null || echo "Old tunnel gone, will use new one"

On a slower connection, this might still time out. But the public reachability test shows the bundle downloads in 26 seconds over the internet. If the user's Wi-Fi is decent, it should work.

If it still doesn't work, the issue is likely:
- T-Mobile CGNAT blocking ngrok
- The phone's Wi-Fi has some filtering

I think the best response is short and direct.
